name: Advanced RAG Architectures
description: Retrieval-Augmented Generation improvements beyond the naive retrieve-then-read pipeline: hybrid search (dense + sparse BM25), re-ranking (cross-encoder models, ColBERT), query expansion and HyDE, multi-vector indexing, and contextual compression. Each component targets a specific failure mode of naive RAG — recall gaps, irrelevant retrieved chunks, semantic mismatch between query and document representation.
contrarian_take: Advanced RAG papers typically ablate individual components in isolation — hybrid search alone, re-ranking alone — but production systems require combining all of them, and the interactions create unexpected failure modes. Adding re-ranking on top of a poor chunking strategy, for example, cannot compensate for fundamentally misrepresented documents. Papers that evaluate full pipeline compositions rather than isolated components reflect how the technology is actually deployed.
example_signals: Precise Zero-Shot Dense Retrieval without Relevance Labels (HyDE)
RAPTOR: Recursive Abstractive Processing for Tree-Organized Retrieval
Lost in the Middle: How Language Models Use Long Contexts
